tool.name: Duplicate file to collection
tool.description: by duplicating a dataset N times

param.name: input
param.label: Input Dataset
param.help: A dataset which will be duplicated.

param.name: number
param.label: Size of output collection
param.help: Number of times to duplicate the input dataset.

param.name: element_identifier
param.label: Element identifier
param.help: Each element in the output collection will be assigned this identifier, with an integer appended.

tool.help:
======== Synopsis ========
Creates a list collection by duplicating a single dataset N times.
=========== Description ===========
This tool takes one input dataset and produces a list collection containing N identical copies of it. You specify the number of copies and a base name for the element identifiers. The copies are numbered automatically — for example, with a count of 3 and base name ``sample``, the output collection will contain ``sample 1``, ``sample 2``, and ``sample 3``.
This is useful when a workflow or tool expects a list collection as input but you only have a single dataset. Instead of uploading the same file multiple times, you can duplicate it into a collection in one step.
======== Examples ========
**Basic duplication**
Given a single reference genome file and the settings:
- *Size of output collection*: ``3`` - *Element identifier*: ``ref``
The output is a list collection containing three identical copies::
List: "reference.fa (duplicated)"
ref 1 → reference.fa ref 2 → reference.fa ref 3 → reference.fa
-------
**Workflow use case**
If you have a list of samples and a tool that compares each sample against a reference, you can duplicate the reference to match the number of samples. Galaxy will then pair them element-wise:
- Sample list: ``[sample1.fq, sample2.fq, sample3.fq]`` - Duplicated reference: ``[ref 1, ref 2, ref 3]`` (all pointing to the same file)
This avoids having to manually create a matched collection.
-------
.. image:: ${static_path}/images/tools/collection_ops/duplicate_file_to_collection.svg :alt: Duplicate file to collection :width: 500
-------
.. class:: infomark
@QUOTA_USAGE_NOTE@
